text: Nearby stars shift as Earth moves—this shift reveals their distance. This phenomenon is called stellar parallax. It's all about perspective. As Earth orbits the Sun, stars appear to slightly shift against the background. Using trigonometry, astronomers calculate distances based on this tiny shift. This method allows us to measure stars thousands of light-years away! Curious about more cosmic mysteries? Like and subscribe for more insights!
